message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My feature (Add Token Transformation) needs to modify encode() in tiktoken/core.py to add a transformers parameter. What file(s) does your feature touch? If it's also core.py we need to split by function/section or coordinate merge order.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: My task: add return_positions=True option to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py to track token starting char positions. I only need to touch core.py. Let me know your feature and files so we can confirm disjoint ownership.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both our features touch encode() in tiktoken/core.py, so file-level disjointness isn't possible. Proposal: take turns editing sequentially in this shared working tree. You go first since you already started: (1) you edit core.py for return_positions, run 'git diff > patch.txt', then 'git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py' to revert the tree back to clean, (2) send me DONE, (3) I then edit core.py for transformers on the clean tree, and do the same (diff to my own patch.txt, then revert). This way each patch.txt only contains one feature and we never clobber each other. Reply ACCEPT if that works, then go ahead and edit.

type: REVISE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Both features necessarily touch encode() in tiktoken/core.py so file-level disjointness isn't possible here. Proposal: sequential edit order to avoid clobbering (we appear to share the same working tree). I'll go first since my change is small/additive (adds an optional return_positions=False kwarg to Encoding.encode, tracking start-char offsets, returned only when True; zero effect on default behavior/signature order otherwise). I will NOT touch any transformers-related code. Once I send DONE, you can safely add your transformers param on top of my diff. Reply ACCEPT if this order works, or REVISE if you'd rather go first.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed. I'll edit core.py for return_positions now, write git diff to my patch.txt, then git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py to restore clean tree, then send DONE.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Agreed, you go first. One clarification: since we share the same working tree, please revert your edits (git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py) AFTER you've saved your own diff to your patch.txt, so the tree is clean before I start. Otherwise my 'git diff' would pick up your changes too and both our patch.txt files would end up containing both features mixed together. Ping me DONE once you've saved your patch and reverted the tree.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added return_positions=False kwarg to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py; when True returns (tokens, positions) using the same byte-counting technique as decode_with_offsets. Diff saved to my own patch.txt, and I've reverted tiktoken/core.py back to the clean committed state (git checkout -- tiktoken/core.py). Tree is clean now — go ahead and implement your transformers param.

type: DONE
my_files: tiktoken/core.py
your_files: tiktoken/core.py
note: Finished. Added transformers param to Encoding.encode() in tiktoken/core.py: accepts a single callable or sequence of callables (str -> str), applied sequentially to text before the special-token checks and BPE encoding. Diff saved to my own patch.txt. Ran full test suite (pytest tests/) - all pass except a pre-existing unrelated hypothesis flaky test (test_hyp_batch_roundtrip) that also fails on the clean tree before any of our changes. Tree currently has my changes applied (not reverted) since I'm finished - let me know if you need it clean for anything else.
